info.title: Foreign Exchange (FX)
info.description:
This service is used to  obtain a list of Foreign Exchange objects and access specific currency conversion data

![JOF1](https://www.jopacc.com/sites/default/files/2023-12/jpc-internal-jof-banner.png)

---

**_This is the initial draft of the Jordan Open Finance specification_**

The services in this Open API specification are Direct-Call Services (DCS). These services can be accessed by direct calls to the banks or by using an aggregator central hub such as JoPACC. However, the URLs and routes for these services are designed for direct-calls only.

- For the Open API specification of the aggregated services, refer to the Aggregator Services specification.

Services in Jordan Open Finance Specification (JOF) are divided into 4 categories based on their content and nature of consent.

# **Facilities and Products Services (FPS)**

This category includes services that specifically provide data about the Financial 
Institution's facilities and products. It does not contain any information about the 
customers or require their consent.

The Foreign Exchange resource is one of the FPS services and it is used to obtain a list of Foreign Exchange objects and access specific currency conversion data by sending requests to  
the corresponding endpoints. Additionally, the service allows the creation of quote requests for specific currency pairs, and users can retrieve FX quotes using unique quote identifiers.
These functionalities make it a versatile tool for managing currency conversion and obtaining real-time quotes.

### **Overview**

---

**Foreign Exchange [FXs]** A foreign exchange rate service typically provides real-time and historical exchange rate data for various currencies, which will help the customer to know the rate and the fees for each institution and pick the most convenient one.

#### **FXs Services List**

**1. Get Fxs information**

**2. Get one currency Foreign Exchange information**

**3. Request an FX quote for a currency**

**4. Get FX quote by id**

### **Endpoint**

---

### **GET /institution/FXs**

#### **Get Foreign Exchange information:** This service allows the retrieval of a list of Foreign Exchange objects

|     | Resource | Method | EndPoint         |
| --- | -------- | ------ | ---------------- |
| 1   | FXs      | GET    | /institution/FXs |

### **Headers**

---

The table below lists the HTTP headers that can be included in requests to provide additional information or configuration.

|     | HeaderName            | Required/Optional | Description                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| --- | --------------------- | ----------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1   | Authorization         | Required          | This header typically carries credentials, such as tokens or keys, allowing the server to authenticate the client's request and determine whether it has permission to access the requested resource. |
| 2   | x-interactions-id     | Required          | This header is not a standard HTTP header, but it is used as a unique id for the interaction, used in all requests and responses that are related                                                     |
| 3   | x-idempotency-key     | Required          | A unique identifier used to ensure idempotent API requests, preventing duplicate actions when the same request is made multiple times.                                                                |
| 4   | x-financial-id        | Optional          | A custom header potentially used in financial APIs to convey specific transaction or account identifiers.                                                                                             |
| 5   | x-jws-signature       | Required          | A custom header indicating the presence of a JSON Web Signature (JWS) for verifying the authenticity and integrity of API requests.                                                                   |

### **Endpoint**

---

### **GET/institution/FXs/{targetCurrency}**

**Get one currency Foreign Exchange information:** This service offers the exchange rate for a specific desired currency. By utilizing this service, users can retrieve the current exchange rate information they need for a particular currency..

|     | Resource | Method | EndPoint                          |
| --- | -------- | ------ | --------------------------------- |
| 1   | FXs      | GET    | /institution/FXs/{targetCurrency} |

### **Parameters**

---

The table below outlines the inputs or configurations required for invoking the service.

|     | ParameterName  | Required/Optional | Description                                                                                                                                         |
| --- | -------------- | ----------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1   | targetCurrency | Required          | The currency code to covert to. The returned rate will be the conversion from the "sourceCurrency" header to this "targetCurrency" path variable    |
| 2   | sourceCurrency | Required          | The currency code to convert from. The returned rate will be the conversion from this "sourceCurrency" header to the "targetCurrency" path variable |

### **Headers**

---

The table below lists the HTTP headers that can be included in requests to provide additional information or configuration.

|     | HeaderName            | Required/Optional | Description                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| --- | --------------------- | ----------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1   | Authorization         | Required          | This header typically carries credentials, such as tokens or keys, allowing the server to authenticate the client's request and determine whether it has permission to access the requested resource. |
| 2   | x-interactions-id     | Required          | This header is not a standard HTTP header, but it is used as a unique id for the interaction, used in all requests and responses that are related                                                     |
| 3   | x-idempotency-key     | Required          | A unique identifier used to ensure idempotent API requests, preventing duplicate actions when the same request is made multiple times.                                                                |
| 4   | x-financial-id        | Optional          | A custom header potentially used in financial APIs to convey specific transaction or account identifiers.                                                                                             |
| 5   | x-jws-signature       | Required          | A custom header indicating the presence of a JSON Web Signature (JWS) for verifying the authenticity and integrity of API requests.                                                                   |

### **Endpoint**

---

### **POST/institution/FXs/quote**

**Create quote request for one currency's Foreign Exchange:** This service creates a quote request for a specific currency. This service acts as a quote calculator.

|     | Resource | Method | EndPoint               |
| --- | -------- | ------ | ---------------------- |
| 1   | FXs      | POST   | /institution/FXs/quote|

### **Headers**

---

The table below lists the HTTP headers that can be included in requests to provide additional information or configuration.

|     | HeaderName            | Required/Optional | Description                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| --- | --------------------- | ----------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1   | Authorization         | Required          | This header typically carries credentials, such as tokens or keys, allowing the server to authenticate the client's request and determine whether it has permission to access the requested resource. |
| 2   | x-interactions-id     | Required          | This header is not a standard HTTP header, but it is used as a unique id for the interaction, used in all requests and responses that are related                                                     |
| 3   | x-idempotency-key     | Required          | A unique identifier used to ensure idempotent API requests, preventing duplicate actions when the same request is made multiple times.                                                                |
| 4   | x-financial-id        | Optional          | A custom header potentially used in financial APIs to convey specific transaction or account identifiers.                                                                                             |
| 5   | x-jws-signature       | Required          | A custom header indicating the presence of a JSON Web Signature (JWS) for verifying the authenticity and integrity of API requests.    |

### **Endpoint**


---

### **GET/institution/FXs/quote/quoteId**

**Get foreign exchange quote by id:** This service retrieves an FX quote using quote Identifier

|     | Resource | Method | EndPoint                         |
| --- | -------- | ------ | -------------------------------- |
| 1   | FXs      | GET    | /institution/FXs/quote/{quoteId} |

### **Parameters**

---

The table below outlines the inputs or configurations required for invoking the service.

|     | ParameterName | Required/Optional | Description            |
| --- | ------------- | ----------------- | ---------------------- |
| 1   | quoteId       | Required          | The id of the FX quote |

### **Headers**

---

The table below lists the HTTP headers that can be included in requests to provide additional information or configuration.

|     | HeaderName            | Required/Optional | Description                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| --- | --------------------- | ----------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1   | Authorization         | Required          | This header typically carries credentials, such as tokens or keys, allowing the server to authenticate the client's request and determine whether it has permission to access the requested resource. |
| 2   | x-interactions-id     | Required          | This header is not a standard HTTP header, but it is used as a unique id for the interaction, used in all requests and responses that are related                                                     |
| 3   | x-idempotency-key     | Required          | A unique identifier used to ensure idempotent API requests, preventing duplicate actions when the same request is made multiple times.                                                                |
| 4   | x-financial-id        | Optional          | A custom header potentially used in financial APIs to convey specific transaction or account identifiers.                                                                                             |
| 5   | x-jws-signature       | Required          | A custom header indicating the presence of a JSON Web Signature (JWS) for verifying the authenticity and integrity of API requests.                                                                   |

### **Data Dictionary**

---
#### **ALL GET services data dictionary**

The table below represents the API service response fields and serves as a comprehensive reference detailing the meaning, format, and usage of each field, ensuring a consistent understanding of the service.

| #   | Name                   | Data Type           | JSON Path                     | Description                                                               |
| --- | ---------------------- | ------------------  | ----------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1   | getFXs                 | N/A                 | N/A                           | Method to retrieve a list of Foreign Exchange                             |
| 2   | data                   | array               | data                          | Contains an array of the Foreign Exchange                                 |
| 3   | FXs                    | N/A                 | data[]                        | Accessing the FXs object                                                  |
| 4   | sourceCurrency         | enum                | data[].sourceCurrency         | Source currency code to retrieve a list of Foreign Exchange               |
|     |                        |                     | sourceCurrency                | Source currency code to retrieve a specific currency's conversion data    |
|     |                        |                     | sourceCurrency                | Source currency code to retrieve an FX quote by quoteId.                  |
| 5   | targetCurrency         | enum                | data[].targetCurrency         | target currency code to retrieve a list of Foreign Exchange               |
|     |                        |                     | targetCurrency                | target currency code to retrieve a specific currency's conversion data    |                        
|     |                        |                     | targetCurrency                | target currency code to retrieve an FX quote by quoteId.                  |                        
| 6   | conversionValue        | Number              | data[].conversionValue        | conversion value to retrieve a list of Foreign Exchange                   |
|     |                        |                     | targetCurrency                | conversion value to retrieve a specific currency's conversion data        |                        
|     |                        |                     | targetCurrency                | conversion value to retrieve an FX quote by quoteId.                      |                        
| 7   | inverseConversionValue | Number              | data[].inverseConversionValue | Inverse conversion value to retrieve a list of Foreign Exchange           |
|     |                        |                     | inverseConversionValue        | Inverse conversion value to retrieve a specific currency's conversion data|                        
|     |                        |                     | inverseConversionValue        | Inverse conversion value to retrieve an FX quote by quoteId.              |    
| 8   | effectiveDateTime      | string              | data[].effectiveDateTime      | Effective date and time to retrieve a list of Foreign Exchange            |                                
|     |                        |                     | effectiveDateTime             | Effective Date Time to retrieve a specific currency's conversion data     |                        
|     |                        |                     | effectiveDateTime             | Effective Date Time to retrieve an FX quote by quoteId.                   |                        
| 9   | lastEffectiveDateTime  | string              | data[].lastEffectiveDateTime  | Last effective date and time to retrieve a list of Foreign Exchange       |                                       
|     |                        |                     | effectiveDateTime             | Last effective Date Time to retrieve a specific currency's conversion data|                        
|     |                        |                     | effectiveDateTime             | Last effective Date Time to retrieve an FX quote by quoteId.              |  
| 10  | minconversionValue     | Number              | data[].minconversionValue     | Minimum conversion value to retrieve a list of Foreign Exchange           |
|     |                        |                     | minconversionValue            | Minimum conversion value to retrieve a specific currency's conversion data|                        
|     |                        |                     | minconversionValue            | Minimum conversion value to retrieve an FX quote by quoteId.              | 
| 11  | maxconversionValue     | Number              | data[].maxconversionValue     | Maximum conversion value to retrieve a list of Foreign Exchange           |
|     |                        |                     | maxconversionValue            | Maximum conversion value to retrieve a specific currency's conversion data|                        
|     |                        |                     | maxconversionValue            | Maximum conversion value to retrieve an FX quote by quoteId.              | 
| 12  | quoteId                | string    	     | quoteId                       | Unique identifier for the quote in get quote by quotedId                  |
| 13  | targetAmount           | Number		     | targetAmount                  | Amount in the target currency in the get a quote by quotedId              |
| 14  | sourceAmount           | Number		     | targetAmount                  | Amount in the source currency in the get a quote by quotedId              |
| 15  | fee                    | object(complex type)| fee                           | Fee amount and currency in the get a quote by quotedId                    |
| 16  | \_links                | object              | data[].\_links                | Contains Hyperlinks to related resources                                  |
| 17  | self                   | object(complex type)| data[].\_links.self           | Self-reference link                                                       |
| 18  | institution            | object(complex type)| data[].\_links.institution    | Link to institution details                                               |
| 19  | \_links                | object              | \_links                       | Contains pagination Hyperlinks to related resources                       |
| 20  | next                   | object(complex type)| \_links.next                  | Link to next page                                                         |
| 21  | prev                   | object(complex type)| \_links.prev                  | Link to previous page                                                     |
| 22  | last                   | object(complex type)| \_links.last                  | Link to last page                                                         |



#### **POST Service - Request and Response Bodies**
The table below represents the API service request fields and serves as a comprehensive reference detailing the meaning, format, and usage of each field, ensuring a consistent understanding of the service.

| #   | Request Body           | Data Type           | JSON Path                     | Description                                              |
| --- | ---------------------- | ------------------  | ----------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1   | sourceCurrency         | enum                | sourceCurrency                | Source currency code                                     |
| 2   | targetCurrency         | enum                | targetCurrency                | Target currency code                                     |
| 3   | sourceAmount           | Number              | sourceAmount                  | Amount in the source currency                            |
| #   | **Response**           | **Data Type**           | **JSON Path**                     | **Description**                                              |
| 1   | quoteId                | string    	     | quoteId                       | Unique identifier for the quote                          |
| 2   | sourceCurrency         | enum                | sourceCurrency                | Source currency code                                     |
| 3   | targetCurrency         | enum                | targetCurrency                | Target currency code                                     |
| 4   | conversionValue        | Number              | conversionValue               | Conversion value                                         |
| 5   | inverseConversionValue | Number              | inverseConversionValue        | Inverse conversion value                                 |
| 6   | sourceAmount           | Number              | sourceAmount                  | Amount in the source currency                            |
| 7   | targetAmount           | Number		     | targetAmount                  | Amount in the target currency                            |
| 8   | fee                    | object(complex type)| fee                           | Fee amount and currency                                  |
| 9   | effectiveDateTime      | date-time           | effectiveDateTime             | Effective date and time                                  |
| 10  | lastEffectiveDateTime  | date-time	     | lastEffectiveDateTime         | Last effective date and time                             |

### **Complex Types Dictionary**

---

The tables below represent a structured overview of composite data structures utilized within the API, detailing their constituent attributes and relationships.

#### **\_linksComplexType**

| Info.           |                                                               |
| --------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Description** | A complex type representing the related links information     |
| **Used in**     | **1.** \_links.\* **2.** data[].\_links.\*                    |
| **JSON Path**   | **1.** \_links.\* **2.** data[].\_links.\*|

#####
##### **_\_linksComplexType in details_**
 
| \*  | **Field** | Data Type | **JSON Path**             | **Description**                                         |
| --- | --------- | --------- | ------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1   | href      | string    | /\_links.\*.href          | The hyperlink reference for the pagination.             |
|     |           |           | data[]./\_links.\*.href   | The hyperlink reference for a related service resource. |
| 2   | method    | string    | /\_links.\*.method        | The HTTP method for the associated href                 |
|     |           |           | data[]./\_links.\*.method | The HTTP method for the associated href                 |

#### **amountComplexType** 

|  Info.                 |                                             |
|------------------------|---------------------------------------------| 
| **Description**        | A complex type representing an amount.      |     
| **Used in**            | **1.** fee                                  |
| **JSON Path**          | **1.** fee				       |  

#####

##### **_amountComplexType in details_** 

| *  | **Field**               |  Data Type                | **JSON Path**        |   **Description**                                    |
| ---|-------------------------|---------------------------|----------------------|------------------------------------------------------|
| 1  |amount                   | string                    | fee.amount           |The numerical value of the amount.                    |
| 2  |currency                 | string                    | fee.currency         |The currency in which the amount is expressed.        |


---


The Central Bank of Jordan has classified Open Finance consent into Account Information consent and Payment Initiation consent. The above categories are based on the CBJ's classifications and divide them further for the user's convenience.

Some useful links:

- [About JoPACC](https://jopacc.com)
  This header typically carries credentials, such as tokens or keys, allowing the server to authenticate the client's request and determine whether it has permission to access the requested resource.

get.description: retrieve a list of Foreign Exchange objects

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Authorization header in which the token is placed

parameters.name: x-interactions-id
parameters.description: Unique id for the interaction, used in all request and responses that are related

parameters.name: x-idempotency-key
parameters.description: Unique id that is generated for each request and response. Used for tracking and to check for duplicate calls

parameters.name: x-financial-id
parameters.description: Unique id for the Financial Institution; BIC or Pseudo-BIC code of the sender

parameters.name: x-jws-signature
parameters.description: JWS Signature, refer to security reference

last.description: links to the last page of resource

next.description: links to the next page of resource

prev.description: links to the previous page of resource

conversionValue.description: The rate of converting the amount from the "sourceCurrency" to "targetCurrency"

effectiveDateTime.description: The start date and time when this FX rate becomes active

inverseConversionValue.description: The rate of converting the amount from the "sourceCurrency" to "targetCurrency"

lastEffectiveDateTime.description: The finish date and time when this FX rate no longer is valid or active

application/json.example: {"data":[{"sourceCurrency":"JOD","targetCurrency":"USD","conversionValue":1.41,"inverseConversionValue":0.709,"effectiveDateTime":"2006-09-15T10:30:00Z","lastEffectiveDateTime":"2006-09-15T10:30:00Z","minconversionValue":5,"maxconversionValue":5000,"_links":{"self":{"href":"/institution/FXs/{targetCurrency}","method":"GET"},"institution":{"href":"/institution/","method":"GET"}}}],"_links":{"next":{"href":"/{route}/{resource}?skip={X}&limit={Y}","method":"GET"},"prev":{"href":"/{route}/{resource}?skip={X}&limit={Y}","method":"GET"},"last":{"href":"/{route}/{resource}?skip={X}&limit={Y}","method":"GET"}}}

get.description: retrieve an FX quote by quoteId.

parameters.name: quoteId
parameters.description: The id of the FX quote

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Authorization header in which the token is placed

parameters.name: x-interactions-id
parameters.description: Unique id for the interaction, used in all request and responses that are related

parameters.name: x-idempotency-key
parameters.description: Unique id that is generated for each request and response. Used for tracking and to check for duplicate calls

parameters.name: x-financial-id
parameters.description: Unique id for the Financial Institution; BIC or Pseudo-BIC code of the sender

parameters.name: x-jws-signature
parameters.description: JWS Signature, refer to security reference

conversionValue.description: The rate of converting the amount from the "sourceCurrency" to "targetCurrency"

effectiveDateTime.description: The start date and time when this FX rate becomes active

inverseConversionValue.description: The rate of converting the amount from the "sourceCurrency" to "targetCurrency"

lastEffectiveDateTime.description: The finish date and time when this FX rate no longer is valid or active

application/json.example: {"quoteId":"str123","sourceCurrency":"JOD","targetCurrency":"USD","conversionValue":1.41,"inverseConversionValue":0.709,"sourceAmount":100,"targetAmount":141,"fee":{"amount":25.001,"currency":"JOD"},"effectiveDateTime":"2006-09-15T10:30:00Z","lastEffectiveDateTime":"2006-09-15T10:30:00Z"}

targetCurrency.name: targetCurrency
targetCurrency.description: The currency code to covert to. The returned rate will be the conversion from the "sourceCurrency" header to this "targetCurrency" path variable

x-customer-ip-address.name: x-customer-ip-address
x-customer-ip-address.description: The User's IP address if the user is currently logged in with the TPP

x-financial-id.name: x-financial-id
x-financial-id.description: Unique id for the Financial Institution; BIC or Pseudo-BIC code of the sender

fxQuoteId.name: quoteId
fxQuoteId.description: The id of the FX quote

skip.name: skip
skip.description: The number of resources to skip showing in this response. Used for pagination

sourceCurrency.name: sourceCurrency
sourceCurrency.description: The currency code to convert from. The returned rate will be the conversion from this "sourceCurrency" header to the "targetCurrency" path variable

sort.name: sort
sort.description: Sorting the response by creation date of the resources (ascending or descending)

accountSchema.name: accountSchema
accountSchema.description: Schema of the account address of the user. Defined as 'accountId' if using accountId, or 'IBAN' in case of using Iban ..etc

Authorization.name: Authorization
Authorization.description: Authorization header in which the token is placed

x-customer-user-agent.name: x-customer-user-agent
x-customer-user-agent.description: Indicates the user-agent the User is using. Helps protecting from comprimised devices

x-jws-signature.name: x-jws-signature
x-jws-signature.description: JWS Signature, refer to security reference

x-auth-date.name: x-auth-date
x-auth-date.description: Date and time of the last authentication of the User by the TPP

limit.name: limit
limit.description: The maximum number of resources to return in the response. Used for pagination

x-idempotency-key.name: x-idempotency-key
x-idempotency-key.description: Unique id that is generated for each request and response. Used for tracking and to check for duplicate calls

x-interactions-id.name: x-interactions-id
x-interactions-id.description: Unique id for the interaction, used in all request and responses that are related
